rulings.Case Title: GARRY EASLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Percipient Witness Phillip Shaw And Production Of Documents At Deposition; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant T.W. Smith Company¿s motion to compel the deposition of Phillip Shaw is denied. While it is commendable that defendant has sought to work with plaintiff¿s counsel to arrange for a convenient date for the deposition of Mr. Shaw, absent a showing that Mr. Shaw is represented by plaintiff¿s counsel or that Mr. Shaw has agreed to abide by scheduling arranged by plaintiff¿s counsel, plaintiff has no legal obligation to make Mr. Shaw available for a deposition. Defendant¿s remedy is subpoena Mr. Shaw. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel R.T. Vanderbilt Company to product outstanding documents in connection with the deposition of R.T. Vanderbilt¿s person most knowledgeable-custodian of records deposition was heard on February 9 and granted by a March 2 order. Unless all issues raised by the motion have been resolved and the parties have so informed the court by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org prior to the hearing, the parties must appear for the compliance hearing. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Further Deposition Of E.I. Du Pont De Nemours And Companys Person Most Knowledgeable-Custodian Of Records And Production Of Documents And Things
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the further deposition of E.I. Du Pone De Nemours. Hearing required unless all issues raised by the motion have been resolved and the parties have so informed the court by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. If not all issues have been resolved before then, no later than 4:30pm today the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, which of the issues addressed in plaintiffs¿ reply memorandum dated February 3, 2011 remain in dispute. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LUISA NICHOLS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Taking Of Depositions Of Defendant Honeywell International, Inc.S Production At Deposition Of Its Person(S) Most Knowledgeable, Custodian Of Records And Requested Documents And Award Of Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,000.00; Declaration Of Rett D.B. Bergmark
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the taking of depositions of Honeywell International, Inc.¿s production at deposition of its person most knowledgeable, custodian of records and requested documents and award of sanctions: Pursuant to CCP 639(a)(5) and CRC 3.920(a), appointment of a discovery referee is warranted. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in an effort to agree on a referee. If the parties cannot agree, each side (plaintiffs being one side and all defendants remaining in the case being one side) must submit the names of three nominees to all parties and the court (by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org) by Wednesday March 9 at 5:00pm. The parties will then have until the hearing at 9:00am on Thursday March 10 to object to the nominees. Each side may object to up to two nominees from the other side. Each nominee should be able to immediately start resolving discovery disputes. This appointment applies to all motions regarding any defendant¿s person most knowledgeable deposition and related document production, as well as any other issues the parties agree to submit to the referee, and any other motions or application later added by court order. Defendants may proceed either through Berry & Berry or on their own. This tentative ruling will apply to all motions filed by plaintiffs regarding defendant¿s PMK deposition that are set to be heard on March 9 and 10. Plaintiffs¿ counsel should already have provided a copy of this tentative ruling to all remaining defendants. = (220/HEK)
